message:
fix(core): remove unused InsertResult::Duplicate variant and clarify cuckoo filter semantics

The Duplicate variant was defined but never returned — cuckoo filters
store fingerprints, not keys, so reliable duplicate detection is
impossible without storing the original keys. The variant's existence
was misleading, suggesting the filter could detect duplicates.

Changes:
- Remove InsertResult::Duplicate from the enum
- Update insert() doc comment to explicitly state that repeated inserts
  are counted separately and that deduplication must happen at a higher
  layer
- Update delete() doc comment to clarify that each delete removes one
  stored fingerprint, requiring matching delete count for repeated inserts
- Rename count() doc: "Number of stored fingerprints" instead of "items"
  to avoid implying unique-item semantics
- Add test repeated_insert_requires_matching_deletes verifying the
  insert-twice/delete-twice contract
- Remove tests referencing the deleted Duplicate variant
- Rename proptest duplicate_inserts_increase_count to
  repeated_inserts_increase_count with updated doc comment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -512,9 +512,10 @@ proptest! {
         prop_assert_eq!(stats.load_percent, expected_percent);
     }
 
-    /// Duplicate inserts increase count each time (cuckoo allows duplicates).
+    /// Repeated inserts increase count each time because the filter stores
+    /// fingerprints, not exact keys.
     #[test]
-    fn duplicate_inserts_increase_count(
+    fn repeated_inserts_increase_count(
         config in arb_large_config(),
         item in 0..100000u64,
         repeats in 2..5usize
